note: Re-baselined 2026-05-22 under stable AC power. The 2026-05-10 recording was contaminated: hgemm/igemm were re-measured this session (median of 7 valid samples each, driver 595.97 / CUDA 13.2) and corrected — hgemm_16warp 2048³ matches old, hgemm_16warp 4096³ is 4.3% below, igemm_sparse_tiled 2048³ is 17% above (old recorded low). igemm_sparse_tiled 4096³ is power-bound on this 150 W laptop and has no fair native-boost baseline (SwPowerCap throttles a varying fraction of the bench's 50 averaged launches); its entry is removed from the automated gate and recorded as a documented reference in docs/inventory.md (50497 dense-equiv GFLOPS at a host-side -lgc lock of 1605 MHz — see scripts/probe/clock_lock_sweep.R). Clock-lock IS available via the Windows-host nvidia-smi.exe (#125 verdict corrected).

default_valid_when.comment: Inherited by every kernel that doesn't set its own valid_when. Override per-kernel for stricter requirements (min_clock_sm, max_temp_c, require_ac).

schema.exe: (optional) path to bench binary; defaults to <kernel-dir>/bench or <kernel-dir>/bench_<basename>
schema.match: (optional) substring of the bench-output line that holds this kernel's number; required when bench.cu prints multiple kernels
schema.section: (optional) substring of a section header; parser only considers lines after this header until the next header
schema.value_label: (optional) text immediately following the throughput number on the matched line; required when the line has multiple numbers
schema.valid_when: (optional) GPU/host state requirement for a fair comparison; refuses to compare if violated. Fields: require_no_throttle (default TRUE), allow_throttle (default ['GpuIdle']), min_clock_sm (MHz), max_temp_c (degC), require_ac (laptop). Tier 10.
schema.tolerance: (optional) per-config tolerance override (fraction). Default is the CLI --tolerance (0.10). Use for kernels that are intrinsically noisy on this hardware.
schema.clock_lock: (optional) integer MHz. Marks a power-bound kernel whose baseline is only fair under a host-side SM clock lock (Windows: nvidia-smi.exe -lgc MHz,MHz, elevated). bench_regress.R SKIPs such a config unless invoked with --clock-locked <MHz> matching this value; when measured it collects a median of CLOCK_LOCK_SAMPLES valid runs and rejects any sample whose observed SM clock leaves the locked band. See docs/benchmark_methodology.md and issue #131.

2048_2048_2048.note: median of 7 valid samples, 2026-05-22, stable AC power, SM clock 1740-1770 MHz boost (spread 30533-31873, ±4%). Matches the 2026-05-10 value (31875) within 0.3% — that recording was not contaminated for this config. Supersedes 0.539 / 31875.

4096_4096_4096.note: median of 7 valid samples, 2026-05-22, stable AC power, SM clock 1770-1785 MHz boost (spread 30005-30569, ±2%). 4.3% below the old 31765 and 1.4% above the 2026-05-21 cold-clock re-measure (29969) — sits between the two. Clean tight measurement; the 4.3% gap is within laptop run-to-run + possible driver delta (595.97). Supersedes 4.327 / 31765.

2048_2048_2048.note: median of 7 valid samples, 2026-05-22, stable AC power, SM clock 1410 MHz (spread 34697-39575). 17% above the old 31588 — that 2026-05-10 value was recorded low (the power-supply fault, fixed mid-recording, depressed the early kernels). Approaches but does not match the CUDA-12.8 reading (0.433 / 39674; new value is 7% below it). Supersedes 0.544 / 31588.

4096_4096_4096.note: Power-bound kernel: it draws >150 W mid-run, so at native boost a varying fraction of the bench's 50 averaged launches hits SwPowerCap, giving a ~1.9x bimodal average with no fair baseline. Measured under a host-side clock lock (Windows nvidia-smi.exe -lgc 1605,1605, elevated): median of 11 valid samples, 2026-05-22, spread 1.02x (within ±5%). 1605 MHz is the highest clock the lock holds cleanly — above it the kernel sags back under the 150 W cap (clock_lock_sweep.R: 1605->50.5k, 1710->50.9k, 1785->SwPowerCap). Gated only when bench_regress.R is run with --clock-locked 1605; SKIPPED otherwise (the pre-push hook does not lock, by design). Supersedes the removed 2026-05-10 power-fault entry (4.449 ms / 30889). See docs/inventory.md and issue #131.
median of 2 valid samples (3 of 5 hit SwPowerCap and were dropped), 2026-05-10. Within 2.5% of CUDA-12.8 baseline (6.6 ms / 20688 TOPS).

1024_8_8.note: median of 5 valid samples, 2026-05-10. +17% throughput vs 2026-05-05 baseline (was 2.810 / 6112) — real improvement from regpv tuning between recordings. Wider tolerance (0.30): this TC-compute-bound kernel is strongly SM-clock-sensitive, and a short single-process bench_regress run cannot drive the laptop GPU to the 1785 MHz boost bin — it caps at 1410 MHz (measured 2026-05-21: 8-run spread 5394-5651 GFLOPS, min/baseline 0.754, exactly the 1410/1785 clock ratio of the 7152 boost-clock baseline; power 43 W, 50 °C, no throttle). Not a code regression. Tolerance matches the igemm_sparse_tiled 4096³ entry (same laptop-artifact pattern). Re-investigate / narrow once clock-locking lands (#125).

1_64_64_320_320.config_note: N=1, H=W=64, Cin=Cout=320; bench shape 'SD 64x64 Cin=Cout=320'
1_64_64_320_320.note: median of 5 valid samples, 2026-05-10. +7% throughput vs 2026-05-05 baseline (was 1.130 / 6687).
